Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 025 [SB Anzu 3][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
oo  (traces of 1 line)
o 2'2'

[x x pa]-an ⸢ša₂-di-i?⸣ [...]

[...] front of the mountain [...].

o 3'3'

[x x] tu₁₅U₁₈.⸢LU⸣ [...]

[...] the south wind [...],

o 4'4'

⸢it-ti⸣ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ x x [...]

with the north wind ... [...].

o 5'5'

it-ti tu₁₅KUR.RA [...]

With the east wind [...],

o 6'6'

it-ti tu₁₅MAR.⸢TU i*-šat* nab*⸣-[li ...]1

with the west wind flaming fire [...].

o 7'7'

i-⸢ša₂⸣-at an-qu-ul-li ⸢uš-ta-na⸣-aḫ-⸢zu⸣2

They keep kindling fire of a fiery glow,

o 8'8'

i-ša₂-⸢at⸣ nab-li ⸢i⸣-qa-⸢mu-šu₂⸣-[nu]

fire of flame burns them.

o 9'9'

⸢i-mur⸣-[ra]-⸢aš₂⸣-šu i-ḫab-⸢bu!⸣-bu ⸢bir⸣-[qu]3

He (Ninurta) saw him (Anzu), as lightning hissed.

o 10'10'

[x (x)] ⸢a⸣-bu-[ba] d⸢NIN⸣.URTA an-[za-a] a-na ⸢ka-ša₂-di⸣

[...] so that the flood of Ninurta reached Anzu,

o 11'11'

[... taq]-rib-ti [kul?]-⸢li⸣ is-⸢sa⸣-[a]

[...] holding a taqribtu-offering he (Ninurta) called out:

o 12'12'

[dDUR?.AN?].⸢KI⸣ re-ša₂-a-⸢su?⸣-[nu] u₂-⸢šaq⸣-[qi]4

'Duranki (Ellil) raised high their top,

o 13'13'

[x x KUR]⸢u₂ ša dNIN⸣.URTA i-da-[a?-šu₂?]

[...] the mountains at Ninurta's side.

o 14'14'

[... d]⸢a⸣-nu [a]-⸢na⸣ e-le-[e]

'In order to go up [...] Anu,

o 15'15'

[...] ⸢e?-te?-li⸣ šad-⸢da⸣-[šu?]

[...] he (Anzu) has gone up his mountain.'

o 16'16'

[x x] ⸢um⸣-man-šu [ša₂?-di?]-⸢iš?⸣-šu ig-⸢gu?⸣-[uš?]

[...] his troops, he went to his mountain,

o 17'17'

[x ḫa-mut-te]-⸢ma qar⸣-[ra]-⸢du⸣ ig-[gu-uš]

[...] haste the hero went to (it).

o 18'18'

[me]-⸢lam-me ez⸣-zu-te x (x) [...]

Furious aura ... [...],

o 19'19'

⸢ḫur-ba⸣-[še] qar-du-te u₂-saḫ-[ḫi-pa x (x)]

chill fear of heroism overwhelmed [...].

o 20'20'

ez-za-⸢at na-mur⸣-ra-ti-šu ⸢ma⸣-[ta] ⸢ik-tum₃⸣

The fury of his awe-inspiring radiance covered the land,

o 21'21'

ra-šub-bat me-⸢lam-me⸣-šu u₂-saḫ-ḫi-pa KUR-MEŠ ⸢nap-ḫar⸣-[ši?]-⸢in?⸣

the terrifying appearance of his aura overwhelmed all the lands.

o 22'22'

ina ⸢ta⸣-ri-ki ⸢IGI⸣-MIN-šu₂ ⸢it⸣-bal-šu

With darkness he took away his (Anzu's) eyes from him,

o 23'23'

[x x] ⸢ša₂⸣ it-⸢bal-šu⸣ [x x]-⸢a?⸣-te e-te-⸢li⸣

[...] that he took away from him went up [...] ....

o 24'24'

[an-za]-⸢a⸣ id-⸢lu!⸣-uḫ-⸢ma⸣ [...] it-⸢ti⸣-kis

He troubled Anzu and cut [...].

o 25'25'

[x ri]-⸢gim⸣ qar-⸢ra-du⸣ i-[ša₂-te] ul-⸢ta-ḫi⸣-iz

[...] the hero's voice, fire was kindled.

o 26'26'

[i-ru-ub]-⸢bu⸣ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ KI⸣[ti₃ x a]-ša₂-red-⸢su⸣-nu5

The gods of the Underworld were trembling [...] their leader,

o 27'27'

[x x a-ra-al]-le ⸢AḪ⸣ [...] ⸢KI?⸣ti₃

[...] the Underworld ... [...] the Underworld.

o 28'28'

[... šu]-bat be-[lu-te-šu] ⸢uḫ-ta⸣-dir

[...] the seat of his lordship became gloomy,

o 29'29'

[... ša]-ar-[ru-ti]-šu [x] x-sa

[...] his kingship [...] ....

Reverse
r 1r 1

[... qar-ra]-⸢di⸣ [i]-⸢ba-aʾ⸣ u₂-x x

[...] the hero went along, ....

r 22

[... in]-⸢da⸣-aḫ ⸢tab⸣-ki-⸢šu₂?⸣-nu6

[...] he kept ... their grain stores,

r 33

[... u₂]-⸢saḫ⸣-ḫi-⸢ip? GUL.GUL*⸣.LA ŠE.⸢GUN₃⸣.NU7

[...] he overwhelmed, he kept destroying the grain crop (saying):

r 44

[e-le-nu]-te ana [šap]-⸢lu-ti!-ma?⸣ aš₂-⸢kun⸣

'I turned the superior people into inferior people,

r 55

[...] ⸢u₂!-šaq-qi₂⸣ re-še-šu-⸢nu⸣

'I raised high their heads [...].

r 66

[x (x)] ⸢il?-ta?-ni?⸣ [(x) (x)] ⸢lil-me⸣-šu₂-⸢nu⸣

'[...] the north wind [(...)] let it surround them,

r 77

[il]-⸢ten₂-ma⸣ a-⸢na pag?-ri?⸣ x [x]-še-e-te-[šu₂?]

'the north wind (is) for the corpses ... his [...] ....

r 88

nap-ḫar pag-⸢ri-šu₂?⸣-[nu? (x) (x)] A x ⸢KI-e⸣-te-⸢šu₂⸣

'All their corpses [(...)] his ...,

r 99

pa-⸢as-ur-ti⸣ [...] ana ⸢ḫa⸣-[mut]-te ⸢il?-li*?-ka⸣8

'news [...] went quickly.

r 1010

⸢lib₃-bi⸣ [li-ku]-⸢un?⸣ x x i-[x] x

'Let my heart be loyal ... [...] ...,

r 1111

⸢ša₂⸣ [ḫi?]-⸢ir⸣-te ⸢AD?⸣ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ u₂⸣-[šap]-⸢ši⸣-[ḫa] x x-⸢sa⸣

'I calmed ... of the spouse of the father of the gods.'

r 1212

[iš]-⸢me⸣-ma [x] ⸢E₂⸣.KUR u₂-[ta]-⸢la!-aṣ⸣

She heard [...] E-kur, so that she was made joyful,

r 1313

[x x] ⸢dNIN⸣.LIL₂ iḫ-[ta]-⸢du⸣-u₂

[...] Ninlil rejoiced:

r 1414

[...] ⸢šu-ru?-uḫ?⸣ [x] ⸢za⸣-kir

'[...] he (Ninurta) is splendid, [...] him be named,

r 1515

[x x] ⸢dEN⸣.LIL₂ be?-⸢li? šum?⸣-šu₂

'as for Ellil [...], My Lord is his name.

r 1616

[x x] x x (x) ⸢ša₂ e-mur-ru?⸣-[šu₂?]9

'[...] ... who saw him,

r 1717

[...] ⸢DA⸣ be-⸢el⸣ ša₂ ⸢DINGIR⸣-[MEŠ?]

'[...] ... lord of the gods,

r 1818

[...] x (x) ša₂ kul-lat x [x]

'[...] ... of all ... [...].'

r 1919

[... dda]-⸢gan a-ši-ib?⸣ [x]

[...] Dagan, inhabitant of [...],

r 2020

[...] ZA [a]-⸢na⸣ EN [x x]

[...] ... to ... [...].

r 2121

[... d]⸢NIN⸣.URTA ⸢i-ri⸣-[šu-ma?]

[...] they rejoiced at Ninurta,

r 2222

[...] x i-⸢pa⸣-[ša₂-ru]

recounting [...] ....

r 2323

⸢ša₂ na⸣-[pi]-⸢iš⸣-ti ⸢an⸣-[ze-e ...]

The one who [...] Anzu's life

r 2424

re-⸢eš₁₅ E₂⸣.KUR ⸢u₂⸣-[ša₂-aṣ-bit-šu₂-nu]

installed them at the top of E-kur.

r 2525

bal-⸢ti⸣ an-zi-i [...]

[...], the dignity of Anzu,

r 2626

x [(x)] ⸢be?-lu⸣-ti il-ka-[kat₃ x x]

... [(...)] of lordship, the mores of [...].

r 2727

[x (x)] x bu-sur [...]

[...] ... [...] was reported,

r 2828

[...] ⸢GIM!⸣ d⸢NIN⸣.[URTA ...]

[...] like Ninurta [...].

r 2929

[...] x-⸢e⸣ [...]

[...] ... [...]

(rest of reverse missing)

1for collation see Vogelzang 1988 p. 127

2presumably for uštanahhazū

3taking verb as a singular ventive in -u

4or re-ša₂-a-su#-[nu] may represent rēšāšunu, 'their heads'

5i-ru-ub-bu may represent irūbū, preterite

6CAD N/I p. 150 nâhu B (meaning unknown); [in]-da#-ah may represent indâh, Gtn preterite

7for collation see Vogelzang 1988 pp. 129-130

8for collation see Vogelzang 1988 p. 130

9ša ēmurrušu could also mean 'whom I/he saw'
